title: deutzia discolor var. albida
intro: Deutzia discolor var. albida, commonly known as the White Deutzia or Pink and White Deutzia, is a flowering plant species in the family Hydrangeaceae. It is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, valued for its fragrant white and pink flowers, which bloom in the spring. Deutzia discolor var. albida has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

The flowers of Deutzia discolor var. albida are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant contain glycosides, including deutzine and deutzol, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and skin conditions. The roots of Deutzia discolor var. albida are also used in traditional medicine and contain iridoids, such as deutzine and deutzol, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
